elements.spec_source: structural sheet S-08 (ขยายคาน คสล.)

elements.description: beam along row F itself (south edge of the F-F' extension bay) -- previously misread as a 'notch beam between F and E'
short vertical extension segment connecting row F to dummy row F' at grid2 -- X-suffix = extension segment beyond the main grid, same pattern as house #2's B2X
short vertical extension segment connecting row F to dummy row F' at grid4, mirroring grid2's

elements.grid_ref: 2 area, near BX callout, between F and E
elements.description: local beam near a small BX-marked opening
interior spine beam along 2 between the large room and middle rooms
corner marker + short column CN at this position

elements.grid_ref: 2 area near D, small local beam near BX callout

elements.description: beam at level between D and C, below the middle room with '1' up-arrow (stair) symbol
vertical beam between middle rooms, D to C

elements.grid_ref: 3-3' x D-C, two stair flights either side of the D'(6.60) lane-divider beam

elements.description: beam at C level over the large lower room, referenced by 'ดูขยาย2' (see expansion detail 2) callout
elements.spec_source: S-08 or a dedicated expansion detail not yet located

elements.grid_ref: B x 4-4' (small stub east of grid4 at row B, 0.60x0.60, SX marker)

elements.description: beam at grid A, drawn with dashed linestyle (possibly indicating a beam below/hidden, or a different floor level)
beam extending south of grid A into a dummy-grid extension zone, dashed linestyle
bottom-most beam of the extension south of grid A, referenced by 'ดูขยาย2' (see expansion detail 2)

slab_markers.grid_ref: 1-2 area near F, small shaft/stair area with diagonal hatching
slab_markers.note: diagonal hatch pattern suggests a stair-up symbol or duct shaft, not confirmed

warnings: This is the largest/most complex beam plan encountered so far in this project (house #5, 224 sqm) -- many interior beam segments (B1/BX/B5/CN cluster around 2-3) are closely spaced and only moderately legible at this image resolution; recorded with best-effort grid_ref positions and confidence 0.55-0.7, flagged for human review rather than guessed with false precision.

RESOLVED (dummy grids now in the master): the southern extension beams (B1 A1-A'1, B2 A'1-A'2) anchor dummy grid A'(-2.05) -- confirmed, ground-floor-only per S-05/M-01. CORRECTED_2026-07-19_by_user_zoom: the top block is a NORTH extension bay F-to-F'(F+1.00, printed '1.00' on the left chain) -- B3 runs along F', B4 along F, B2X are the 1.00m vertical extension segments at grids 2/4 (elements updated); previously misread as a 'notch between F and E'. Also per Makham's beam-endpoint rule: BX1 stub anchors new dummy 4'(9.6); the stairwell-zone interior beams (two stacked S1 'rooms' 3-4 x D-C with off-grid B1 divider and off-grid B2 east wall = stair landing beams per S-05's ชานพัก callouts) still REQUIRE dummy lines whose positions are not printed on this sheet -- get dims from stair details (S-11/S-12) before adding. The right-hand SO carport slab edge at A-1.50 (printed '1.50') has NO beam -> deliberately not a dummy grid.

A dashed linestyle is used for several beams in the southern half of the plan (B2 at grid A, B1/B2 in the extension zone) -- dashed usually means 'beam below current floor / not visible at this cut level' in these drawing sets, but this hasn't been cross-confirmed against the legend on S-01; recorded as printed.

The 0.60m/0.50m overhangs beyond 1/4 at the top dimension chain (F row) appear to be eave/slab-edge dimensions rather than a beam-bearing dummy grid, since no beam is drawn beyond those points at the F line itself -- unlike the southern extension which does show real beams. Flagged as a lower-priority discrepancy vs. the southern extension.
